result:
Build is green — no errors, only pre-existing warnings.

**Root cause:** `LuaStateStubExt::load_buffer_ex` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:977` ignored its `mode` parameter (`let _ = mode;`) and passed `None` to `lua_vm::api::load`. So `load(x, "modname", "b")` on line 317 of `calls.lua` reached `f_parser`'s `check_mode(..., None, b"text")`, which is a no-op when mode is `None` — the text chunk loaded successfully instead of erroring with `"attempt to load a text chunk"`. `cannotload`'s `assert(not a and ...)` then failed because `a` was a valid function.

**Fix:** Added the `M: AsRef<[u8]>` bound to `load_buffer_ex` (both the trait default at line 181 and the impl at 977 — matching the existing `load_with_reader` pattern) and changed the impl to pass `Some(mode.as_ref())` through to `lua_vm::api::load`. The downstream `protected_parser` → `f_parser` → `check_mode` chain already correctly raises a syntax error when mode is `b"b"` and the chunk begins with a non-`\x1b` byte.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` succeeds. Did not re-execute the test (per instructions); mega-loop will re-test. The line-317 assertion should now succeed since `load(x, "modname", "b")` will return `(nil, "...attempt to load a text chunk (mode is 'b')...")`.
